data.title: Eregli Demir ve Çelik Fabrikalari T.A.S. reports FY result
data.description:

  • Revenue of TRY 208.9B (+2.4% Y/Y).

data.title: Raspberry Pi stock builds on 42% spike amid AI buzz
data.description:
• Raspberry Pi (RPBPF) shares continued their rally on Wednesday after jumping as much as 42% on Tuesday, marking a record two-day gain. The surge followed CEO Eben Upton’s stock purchase, which halted a months-long slide amid speculation that the company’s products could see increased demand from low-cost AI projects.
  • Two London traders, according to Reuters, said the driver behind the surge was not clear, though the move followed a filing showing Upton bought about 13,224 pounds worth of shares at around 282 pence each on Monday. 
  • The stock is still about 50% below a record high hit a year ago. Raspberry Pi was up 21% in London at press time.
  • X  user @aleabitoreddit, who has more than 58,000 followers, posted on Monday, “Fun Trade Idea: Long $RPI (Raspberry Pi),” claiming buyers have recently begun hoarding the company's devices because they are far cheaper than “$500‑plus Apple products.”

data.title: BAE Systems plc Non-GAAP EPS of 75.20p, revenue of £30.66B; provides FY26 outlook
data.description:
• BAE Systems plc press release (BAESY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 75.20p.
  • Revenue of £30.66B (+8.2% Y/Y). Organic growth was 9%.
  • Underlying EBIT increased by 12%2 with a 20bps increase in return on sales from 10.6% to 10.8%.
  • Free cash flow was strong at £2,158M.
  • Order backlog grew by £5.8B to a record £83.6B. Order intake was strong at £36.8B.
  • The board has recommended a final dividend of 22.8p, taking the total dividend for 2025 to 36.3p - an increase of 10% on last year. Subject to shareholder approval, the final dividend will be paid on 4 June 2026 to shareholders on the share register on 24 April 2026.

data.title: European markets rise over improved market sentiment
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.53% to 10,612. UK's inflation eases to 3% in January. The British pound traded below $1.36 as investors assessed fresh inflation data.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.52% to 25,131. 

France (CAC:IND) +0.33% to 8,388. France inflation cools in January.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.53% higher to around €625, moving closer to record levels with strong gains seen in defense, and markets reacted to a possible leadership change at the European Central Bank. 

In corporate earnings, defense stocks jumped, led by a nearly 6% surge in BAE Systems (BAESY) after the company reported a stronger-than-expected rise in full-year operating profit. Sentiment was also supported by reports that Germany is considering taking a minority stake in European defense company KNDS.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.08%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 2.75%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 4.39%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Spark New Zealand reports H1 results
data.description:
• Spark New Zealand press release (SPKKY):  Reported revenue of $1,893M declined 1.2%.
  • Adjusted revenue of $1,917M declined 1.1%
  • Reported EBITDAof $448m increased 10.3%
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $471M increased 5.1%
  • H1 FY26 dividend of 8 cents per share declared
  • Guidance 2026: Adjusted EBITDAI: $1,010 million-$1,070 million • Free cash flow: $290 million-$330 million • BAU capital expenditure: $380 million-$410 million • Strategic capital expenditure (data centres): ~$55 million • Dividend payout ratio: 100% of FY26 free cash flow

data.title: Portland General prices stock offering at $50.70
Lagarde said to weigh quitting ECB before her term ends
data.description:
European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde is weighing stepping down from her role before the end of her term in October 2027, the Financial Times reported, citing a person familiar with her thinking.

Lagarde reportedly wants to exit the ECB before the French presidential election in April 2027, although it's unclear when she will leave.

The ECB chief wants outgoing French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz to decide on her successor, the person with knowledge of her thinking told FT.

"President Lagarde is totally focused on her mission and has not taken any decision regarding the end of her term," a spokesperson for the ECB said.

Pablo Hernández de Cos and Klaas Knot, former heads of the Spanish and Dutch central banks, respectively, were viewed as top picks to succeed Lagarde, according to an FT poll of European economists in December.
